fundamentals.name: Reddit, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Reddit, Inc. operates a digital community in the United States and internationally. The company's platform enables user to engage in conversations, explore passions, research new hobbies, exchange goods and services, create new communities and experiences, share laughs, and find belonging. It also organizes communities based on specific interests that enable users to engage in conversations by sharing experiences, submitting links, uploading images and videos, and replying to one another. Reddit, Inc. was founded in 2005 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high margins and a strong balance sheet, trading at a reasonable forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 79.1%; next earnings date is 2026-07-30.
positives: Revenue growth 69.1% YoY and earnings growth 675.0% YoY indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin 91.4% and profit margin 28.6% demonstrate excellent operational efficiency.
Forward P/E of 16.37 is attractive relative to the company's high growth rate and trailing P/E of 41.31.
Total cash of $2.77B significantly outweighs total debt of $21.29M, indicating a very healthy balance sheet.
Free cash flow of $564.09M TTM shows robust cash generation.
negatives: Price/Sales (TTM) of 11.25 is on the higher side, suggesting a premium valuation based on revenue.
Beta of 1.85 indicates higher volatility compared to the broader market.
The significant pullback in share price suggests market uncertainty or a repricing of growth expectations.

news.paragraph: Reddit's valuation is under scrutiny following a recent share price pullback, with conflicting signals on its fair value. While the company has shown impressive revenue and earnings growth, the market is grappling with how to price its rapid expansion. The upcoming earnings report on July 30th will be a key event to watch for further clarity on its trajectory.

headlines.title: Reddit (RDDT) Valuation Check After Share Price Pullback And Conflicting Fair Value Signals
